chunks.text: THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Present: Mr. Justice Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry Mr. Justice Muhammad Jaffer Raza C. P. No. D-5194 of 2025 Muhammad Shafique V. Government of Sindh & anther Petitioner: Muhammad Shafique through Mr. Ghulam Abbas Advocate. Date of hearing: 24-10-2025 Date of decision: 24-10-2025 O R D E R Muhammad Jaffer Raza, J,. Through the instant petition, the Petitioner seeks a writ against the elections of the Pakistan Blind Association ("Association"). It has been contended by the learned counsel that the elections are to be held on 25.10.2025 and he apprehends that the process will be contrary to the by-laws of the Association. Through the instant petition a thorough inquiry and independent audit of all financial accounts of the Association is also sought. We have specifically confronted the learned counsel as to how the instant petition is maintainable against the Association1 and the private Respondents, under Article 199 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973. The learned counsel in this regard was unable to render any assistance or identity any provision of law which would warrant invoking the writ jurisdiction of this court. In the light of the same, it is held that the instant petition is devoid of merit and the same is dismissed in limine. J U D G E J U D G E Arshad/ 1 PAKISTAN OLYMPIC ASSOCIATION through President and others Versus NADEEM AFTAB SINDHU and others reported at 2019 SCMR 221.
